title: Start-to-Start and Finish-to-Finish task dependencies
excerpt: I’m working on converting my team to Monday from MS Project, and one of their chief requests is the S2S and F2F feature that Project has. I don’t see a way to set that up with Monday’s dependencies column. Has anyone wor…

posts.author: BriKHami (Brik )
posts.cookedHtml: I’m working on converting my team to Monday from MS Project, and one of their chief requests is the S2S and F2F feature that Project has. I don’t see a way to set that up with Monday’s dependencies column. Has anyone worked out how to replicate this in Monday?

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @BriKHami

Looks like this is work in progress. In one of my account I do see this:

image510×531 25.9 KB

Although this is good progress I still believe the app SchedulerPro from Work Perfect is the best solution for all 4 dependency types, also because it offers very flexible workweek and holiday schedules which will be skipped in the planning.

image1017×1115 68.9 KB

posts.author: kolaai (alfred)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @BriKHami,

Another alternative is the Date Check app which supports all the four main dependency types and also handles non-working days, country holidays and days off for users in users in your account when calculating for durations.
Here is a demo of the app in action.

image935×526 33.7 KB

Additionally, here are some other features of the app:
Has built-in holiday checker for most countries

Dependency Types support (Finish to start (FS), Finish to finish (FF), Start to start (SS), Start to finish (SF))

monday.com Gantt view support

Templates can be used to check for time off for employees in your account

Automatic notification, date and timeline update when users are assigned tasks on their days off or on public holidays

Templates created can be used on any board

Templates are triggered by native monday.com automations

Any trigger can be used to check for holidays in dates and timelines

Multiple custom configurations

posts.author: ZNovkovic (Zdenko Novkovic)
posts.cookedHtml: i am having the same issue. the finish/start dependencies are not functioning correctly.
